title: Destroy Malevolence
content:
{{Top|rwm|can|fotj}} {{Redlink}} {{TelevisionEpisode |image=[[File:Destroy_Malevolence.jpg]] |title="Destroy Malevolence" |series=''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode004.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Destroy ''Malevolence''}} |season=[[The Clone Wars: Season One|One]] |episode=4 |production=1.11 |screener= |airdate=[[October 17]], [[2008]]{{ThisWeek|DpATvwrcrUw|The Mandalorian Character Posters, Life Day Celebrations with LEGO, and More!|sw_url=video/the-mandalorian-character-posters-life-day-celebrations-with-lego-and-more}} |intl release= |runtime=24 minutes |director=[[Brian Kalin O'Connell]] |writer=*[[Steven Melching]] *[[Scott Murphy]] {{C|Supervising Writer}}{{TCW|Destroy Malevolence|Destroy ''Malevolence''}} *[[Henry Gilroy]] {{C|Staff Writer}} |production company= |guests= |canon_timeline=[[22 BBY]]{{TCWcanondates|104}} |legends_timeline=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]{{TCWdates|104}} |prev="[[Shadow of Malevolence|Shadow of ''Malevolence'']]" |next="[[Rookies (episode)|Rookies]]" }} {{Quote|A plan is only as good as those who see it through.|Moral|}} "'''Destroy ''Malevolence'''''" is the fourth episode of [[The Clone Wars: Season One|Season One]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series. It is the fourth episode of the series overall, and concludes a three-part storyline. It first aired on [[October 17]], [[2008]] on [[Cartoon Network]]. Its title was first revealed on [[Anthony Daniels]]' website. ==Official description== ''Padmé Amidala and C-3PO are taken hostage by General Grievous, leaving Anakin and Obi-Wan to save the Senator and complete the destruction of the ''Malevolence''. Anakin and Obi-Wan chase down the ''Malevolence'', hoping to destroy it before it can escape. But when Padmé and C-3PO are captured and held hostage aboard, the Jedi are forced to hatch a new plan.'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode 4
DESTROY MALEVOLENCE |text=Grievous in retreat! Before the battleship Malevolence could destroy an Outer Rim clone medical base, a Republic strike force, under the command of Jedi General Anakin Skywalker, crippled the warship, disabling its dreaded ion cannon. Now the Jedi relentlessly pursue the Malevolence....}} ==Plot summary== {{Quote|Continue your attack! You ''must'' destroy this monstrous ship!|Padmé Amidala, to the commanders assembled aboard the ''Resolute''| audio=SeparatistBargainingChip-DM.flac|}} Heavily damaged during the [[Battle of the Kaliida Nebula]], the ''[[Malevolence]]'', under the command of [[Grievous|General Grievous]], flees, pursued by three Republic [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|attack cruisers]]. Due to the ''Malevolence''{{'s}} size, the cruisers are limited in the damage they can cause. [[Jedi Master]] [[Plo Koon]] suggests summoning reinforcements. [[Obi-Wan Kenobi|General Kenobi]] asks about Jedi Master [[Luminara Unduli]], but [[Anakin Skywalker]] tells him that she is currently engaged with a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatist]] fleet and unable to lend support. Believing the ''Malevolence''{{'s}} [[hyperdrive]] is damaged, General Kenobi orders all guns to target the ''Malevolence''{{'s}} bridge. [[Count]] [[Dooku]] contacts Grievous and informs him that a trap has been set to give Grievous an advantage over the Jedi. [[Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala]] is currently en route to the ''Malevolence'' under false pretenses. Capturing her and holding her hostage should convince the Republic to cease their attack. Senator Amidala travels through [[hyperspace]] in a [[H-type Nubian yacht|Nubian yacht]], accompanied by her droid companion [[C-3PO]]. Per information given to her by [[Darth Sidious|Chancellor Palpatine]], the senator believes she is rendezvousing with the Supreme Executive of the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan]] at a clandestine point in space to negotiate a treaty. Amidala's ship emerges from hyperspace directly in the path of the ''Malevolence'', barely avoiding a collision. [[Ahsoka Tano]] sees the ship on the scanners, and Anakin tells her to contact the ship. Padmé answers, identifying herself and explaining to them her situation, imploring them to continue their attack. Anakin, however, orders the attack to stop. Lamenting her gullibility, Padmé sets her ship's power systems to overload then flees with C-3PO. Grievous arrives in the hangar and boards Padmé's ship just before it explodes. He is caught in the blast, but survives. He orders his droids to search for the stowaways. Anakin, Obi-Wan, and [[R2-D2]] mount a rescue mission and fly the ''[[Twilight]]'' toward the ''Malevolence'', hoping the battleship's sensors are too damaged to notice their approach. They dock with an emergency airlock and make their way on board. Padmé contacts Anakin, and they arrange to rendezvous at a central location along the battleship's rail jet system. At the rendezvous, Amidala and C-3PO are spotted by a passing droid patrol and are forced backward onto separate rail jets heading in opposite directions. Anakin spots Padmé and jumps onto a passing rail jet running alongside hers. Obi-Wan goes after 3P0. A [[B2-HA super battle droid|super battle droid]] destroys the track in front of Padmé, and Anakin uses [[the Force]] to safely pull Padmé aboard his rail jet. As the two are about to enjoy a romantic moment, Obi-Wan contacts Anakin to tell him he's been separated from C-3PO. Anakin says he'll go after the droid, but Padmé informs the Jedi that she overheard Grievous and that the ship's hyperdrive is close to being repaired. Obi-Wan pledges to destroy the hyperdrive. Monitoring the ship's internal communications, Grievous overhears this plan and sends droids to guard the drive. Obi-Wan [[Lightsaber duel|duels]] Grievous after dispatching his droids and avoids the cyborg's clutches. Meanwhile, Anakin and Padmé reach the bridge when the ship is almost ready to jump to hyperspace. They quickly destroy all the droids and rig the ship's [[Navigation computer|navicomputer]]. They then leave the bridge after hiding the broken droids. Obi-Wan reaches the ''Twilight'' along with the others and, as they leave, Grievous pursues in [[Soulless One|his starfighter]]. The droids on the ''Malevolence'' try to jump to hyperspace, only to discover that Anakin rigged the navicomputer to set a course into the moon. Grievous watches in horror as the ship crashes into a nearby moon, then flees the system. While Grievous was not killed on the ''Malevolence'', his warship is finally destroyed, and Senator Amidala is rescued from the ship. The loss hurts Grievous's credibility with Count Dooku, the former ending communications with the Count due to the shame of the ship being destroyed. ==Continuity== Some ideas from the original script of the episode included [[Quarsh Panaka]] being captured alongside Padmé and being brutally [[Death|murdered]] by [[Grievous]], a [[lightsaber duel]] between Grievous and Plo Koon on the exterior of ''[[Malevolence]]'' in [[Realspace|space]], and [[Jedi]] sneaking aboard the ''Malevolence'' by [[Carbon-freezing|freezing]] themselves in [[carbonite]] to get past droid bioscans and radiation beams.{{WebCite|url=http://theforce.net/jedicouncil/interview/henrygilroyanddavefiloni.asp|text=Face To Face With The Masters|partialdate=2008-12|work=TheForce.net|archivedate=20200112170948}} While the first two ideas were abandoned completely, the carbonite infiltration maneuver was later adapted into the stories of ''[[The Clone Wars: Shipyards of Doom]]'' and the season 3 episode "[[The Citadel (episode)|The Citadel]]." Additionally, Anakin's original plan of entering the ''Malevolence'' was a foolish hyperspace [[microjump]] to deploy the ''Twilight'' [[centimeter]]s from the ship's hull. Padmé and C-3PO were also originally rushing to the battle site to aid in the evacuation of the [[Kaliida Shoals Medical Center]], but these were also changed after script revisions. When General Grievous and his droids surround Obi-Wan near the hyperdrive, he says "[[Hello there]]." Obi-Wan would later repeat this line to the General in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], to a young Luke Skywalker in ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', and to R2-D2 during his debut appearance in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. General Grievous said "I will deal with this Jedi myself" in ''Revenge of the Sith'', at the [[Battle of Utapau]]. Anakin's comments about spinning "being a good trick" are references to his line in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The final frame of the episode shows all major [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]] characters (Anakin, Obi-Wan, Padmé, C-3PO, and R2-D2) depicted together for the first time. (They were scattered across different [[planet]]s in the films.) ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Destroy Malevolence}} ===Collected in=== *''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: A Galaxy Divided]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season One|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' The Complete Season One]] ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= *'''[[Matt Lanter]]''' as '''[[Anakin Skywalker]]''' *'''[[James Arnold Taylor]]''' as '''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]''', '''[[Plo Koon]]''' *'''[[Catherine Taber]]''' as '''[[Padmé Amidala|Padme Amidala]]''' *'''[[Matthew Wood]]''' as '''[[General]] [[Grievous]]''', '''[[B1-series battle droid|Battle Droids]]''' *'''[[Anthony Daniels]]''' as '''[[C-3PO]]''' *'''[[Ashley Eckstein]]''' as '''[[Ahsoka Tano]]''' *'''[[Dee Bradley Baker]]''' as '''[[Clone trooper|Clone Troopers]]''' *'''[[Tom Kane]]''' as '''[[Admiral]] [[Wullf Yularen|Yularen]]''', '''Narrator''' *'''[[Corey Burton]]''' as '''[[Count of Serenno|Count]] [[Dooku]]''' *'''[[Olivia d'Abo]]''' as '''[[Luminara Unduli]]''' *'''[[Paul Dini]]''' – Additional Dialogue *'''[[Dave Filoni]]''' – Additional Dialogue |crew= *'''[[George Lucas]]''' – Creator, Executive Producer *'''[[Catherine Winder]]''' – Producer *'''[[Dave Filoni]]''' – Supervising Director *'''[[Brian Kalin O'Connell]]''' – Director *'''[[Steven Melching]]''' – Writer *'''[[Scott Murphy]]''' – Supervising Writer *'''[[Henry Gilroy]]''' – Staff Writer *'''[[Kevin Kiner]]''' – Composer *'''[[John Williams]]''' – Original Star Wars Themes and Score *'''[[Sarah Wall]]''' – Associate Producer *'''[[Athena Yvette Portillo]]''' – Associate Producer *'''[[Andrew Harris]]''' – CG & Lighting Supervisor *'''[[Paul Zinnes]]''' – Digital Set Supervisor *'''[[Tim Brock]]''' – Design and Concept Artist, Texture Artist *'''[[Russell G.
